fields.description:
## Why
The DET floor is the ONLY blocking tier (every LLM criterion is advisory in v1). It must be a frozen,
deterministic, polyglot floor that fails open on any unsupported stack.

## What
- P1 readiness-shape; P2 file/symbol/import resolution (universal-ctags via the 8f6c oracle — LSP/solidlsp is the T2 follow-on `850f`, NOT v1); P3 package existence
  (deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM); P4 oversize (scc/lizard metrics); P5 task-DAG validity + interference;
  P6 AC/DD quality; P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates T4). The polyglot resolution/dependency/size tooling (P2/P3/P4) is CONSUMED — via the SHIPPED `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan` facade — from the now-CLOSED prerequisite epic 8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82 (code-grounding oracle, `src/rebar/grounding/`) — this child does NOT re-build it; it owns the rebar-specific checks (P1/P5/P6/P7) + wiring P2/P3/P4 into the DET floor.

## Scope
- Call the 8f6c grounding-oracle facade (refute_absence/applies/scan); its three-valued abstain-by-default contract IS the fail-open guarantee — unsupported lang / no tool / parse-error / timeout -> abstain (recorded reason) -> skipped = PASS (ironclad).
- The shared oracle SHIPPED as prerequisite epic 8f6c (CLOSED; also consumed by code-review `9da1`); this child depends_on it and consumes it as a library, not as new integrations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P1-P7 implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open (skipped=pass) tested per tool.
- [ ] Coverage recorded on the attestation.
- [ ] DET floor is the only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1 (every LLM criterion is advisory; per-criterion LLM-tier blocking is OPT-IN via its `block_threshold` — 'DET-only' is the v1 default posture, not an architectural limit).

## (Added) P8 — reviewability / context-budget (DET)
A meta-finding, not tied to any one criterion, so it lives in the deterministic floor as P8: a DET check that
PASSES unless the ticket content (or a parent+child pairing for container criteria) EXCEEDS — or is
anticipated (token-estimate) to exceed — the largest configured context window even at one-criterion-per-call.
In that case it FAILS with 'ticket too big to review in full; reduce/decompose it' (the extreme of P4 oversize
/ G5 decomposition). Deterministic (a size/estimate check, no LLM); the orchestrator emits it during the
size-handling ladder.
## Acceptance Criteria (added)
- [ ] P8 reviewability DET check: passes unless content (or a parent+child pairing) exceeds — or is anticipated to exceed — the largest context window at one-criterion-per-call; else a 'ticket too big, reduce it' failure finding.

## Acceptance Criteria (added — T8 DET execution path)
- [ ] P1–P8 run as the deterministic `exec=DET` tier the orchestrator dispatches via its code executor (no LLM) — an explicit, first-class execution path, not merely a named tier.
